entities.name: Plan B Media Public Company Limited

financial.note: VGI holds ~24.13% stake (as of 2026). The partnership allows for cross-platform sales management; VGI's financial health and BTS ridership directly impact PLANB's managed media revenue.
Acts as registrar for the SME26NA and SME27DA series; rising interest rates increase the cost of capital for their aggressive ฿4bn acquisition of Hello LED.
Serves as registrar for the ฿6.0bn 2026 debenture series; PLANB's refinancing capability is sensitive to GSB's credit terms.
Holds ~23.82% stake; strategic direction and majority control over 'Engagement Marketing' pivots (Boxing, Olympics) remain with the founder.

supply_chain.note: PLANB manages the sales and marketing for VGI's entire OOH portfolio; a significant portion of 'Transit' revenue is derived from this agency-style agreement.
PLANB holds 20% of BMN; commercial rights for MRT Blue Line media are critical for the 'Transit' segment revenue floor.
Exclusive provider of digital screens inside ~14,000 7-Eleven stores; success depends on foot traffic and CPALL's outlet expansion.
Holds exclusive management rights for FAT and Thai League until 2028; performance is tied to the commercial success of national football.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Acquired for ฿4bn in 2025; consolidates market leadership in static and digital billboard segments.
Manages BNK48/CGM48; provides the foundation for the 'Artist Management' revenue stream.

competitors.note: While allies, they are the two dominant players for OOH budgets; VGI focuses on the BTS ecosystem, while PLANB dominates billboards and retail.
Competes in provincial static media; PLANB has largely neutralized this threat through Resell agreements.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: Electoral spending in early 2026 drives a surge in demand for static billboards and transit media across the country.
As a rights manager for international sports broadcasting, PLANB captures a major revenue spike from sponsorships and airtime sales.
Forecasted 35.5M arrivals in 2026 drive occupancy and spending in 'Airport' and 'Retail' media segments (Mega Bangna, Central malls).
High debt (~90%) constrains consumer goods (FMCG) advertising budgets, which are the primary source of OOH spending.
